incorporated: increase development evaluation from 1024 to 4096 paired games
remove the arbitrary hidden 60% absolute-win cutoff
center PPO clipping on the frozen V30 anchor while retaining teacher behavior correction
split V30 training parity from report-only V23 baseline context
pin strategic and hybrid sampled-row contracts
restrict trust decisions to the V30 selection half and preserve the 0.40 strategic-p99 safety margin
use simultaneous intervals as the causal multiplicity gate and keep McNemar/Holm informational
add anchor-ratio, clipping, and strategic-entropy telemetry

scope: Causal screen of conservative long-horizon solo credit from a gate-valid entity-v2 anchor. This stage can fund hidden solo confirmation and on-policy PPO, but cannot promote a production bot.
hypothesis: V23 trajectories contain enough paired state, sampled-action, behavior-probability, and terminal-success information to improve late-game strategic choices with a small clipped off-policy policy-gradient term. Retaining the exact teacher mean-KL and strategic-tail CVaR anchors should prevent the outcome signal from erasing the strong early-game policy.

train.dataPolicy: Frozen V23 at temperature 0.55; outcomes and behavior log-probabilities are immutable.

fidelityValidation.use: Trust-region monitoring only on the former V30 selection half. V31 has fixed epochs and no validation-based checkpoint or coefficient selection; the former V30 one-shot gate half is not reused for a decision.

strategicPredicate.definition: A decision row is strategic when any legal candidate has type lockNavigation or selectNavigationDestination. The default navigation scope is used; engine-cycle types are not included.

implementation.hybridSamplingContract: Hybrid deterministic immediate-VP/win branches have policyMask=0 and no logpOld, so they remain critic/trajectory states but receive zero terminal policy credit. Only policyMask=1 rows observed inside an exact sample=true learned-policy pick enter teacher KL or terminal credit. Teacher, anchor, and current student probabilities are all recomputed at behaviorTemperature 0.55 on the identical recorded behaviorMask support.

estimator.advantage: reach30Target minus clamp(frozen behavior reach30Pred, 0.05, 0.95) on strategic policyMask=1 rows only
estimator.behaviorCorrection: rho0 = exp(frozenAnchorChosenLogp - frozenTeacherChosenLogp), detached and upper-truncated at 2.0
estimator.loss: -equal_game_mean(rho0 * min(r * advantage, clip(r, 0.8, 1.2) * advantage)), using the sign-correct PPO minimum for both advantage signs
estimator.reason: The teacher remains the actual sampled behavior denominator, while PPO clipping is centered at the policy being updated. This avoids making outcomePgCoef an asymmetric teacher-restoration coefficient.
estimator.behaviorCorrectionCaveat: Upper truncation reduces variance and introduces conservative bias. Report the untruncated distribution and capped fraction; do not tune the cap after training starts.

requiredTestsBeforeTraining: positive- and negative-advantage clipping has the expected loss and gradient direction
zero-weight and non-strategic rows have zero outcome gradient
each complete game contributes total terminal-credit weight one regardless of trajectory length
policyMask=0 hybrid deterministic rows never enter behavior correction or terminal credit
teacher, anchor, and student chosen probabilities use the exact same recorded support and temperature
coefficient zero is numerically identical to the V30 objective under the same seed and batch
every trust metric is checked after every epoch and a breached arm cannot be rescued by a later checkpoint
all arm initial checkpoints are byte-identical and train/validation/development seed sets are disjoint
implementation.preflight: Run audit-only against the merged train and validation data before constructing an optimizer. Require exact schemas, complete games, finite fields, equal-game weights, and teacher chosen-logp reconstruction error <= 0.001.
telemetry: base teacher mean KL, strategic-tail CVaR loss, and outcome surrogate separately

outcome advantage, untruncated and truncated anchor/teacher behavior ratio, current/anchor update ratio, cap fraction, PPO clip fraction, and effective strategic-row counts split by win/loss, advantage sign, and round band
per-epoch overall and strategic fidelity including mean, p99, CVaR@5%, max, and top-1 agreement
teacher, frozen-anchor, and current-student strategic policy entropy
training wall time, peak RSS, exit code, checkpoint hash, stats hash, and exact command

preflightResult.conclusion: The anchor/teacher correction is centered at one, its p99 remains below the fixed 2x cap, and only 0.53% of strategic rows are capped. Proceed with the frozen estimator and do not retune the cap.

training.capacityDecision: Keep architecture fixed so the terminal-credit mechanism is causal. V27-V30 showed that width was not the fidelity or latency bottleneck; reconsider width only after a valid on-policy/search result identifies capacity as limiting strength.

shared.save: Last epoch only; no early stopping or checkpoint selection

training.computeMatching: All arms load byte-identical weights and execute identical data, batch order, teacher forwards, optimizer steps, fidelity terms, and validation passes. Only outcomePgCoef differs.

trustGateEveryEpoch.rule: Any non-finite statistic or threshold breach immediately and permanently invalidates that arm. Do not restore an earlier epoch.

developmentEvaluation.openOnlyAfter: All arm checkpoints, manifests, stats, exit codes, and hashes are frozen and every valid arm passes the per-epoch trust gate.

attempts.failure: All five immediate evaluators failed at Node mkdtemp because the host root filesystem backing /tmp had zero free bytes. The chained sixth arm exited 99 after its dependency failed.
attempts.evidence: mkdtemp occurs before runActorPool in scripts/evaluate-solo-checkpoint.mjs; no report.json exists for any policy and no actor pool or game started.
attempts.repair: Bind each evaluator's TMPDIR and inference socket to experiment-owned /dev/shm/arc-v31-development scratch, require at least 8 GiB free, preserve this failed attempt, then resume the unchanged preregistered seed block.

developmentEvaluation.fairness: Use identical contiguous seeds and decoding semantics for every policy. V2 policies use the production binary inference path with policyObsVersion 2; V23 uses its native obs-v1 path. Record catalog and checkpoint hashes and require zero stalls.

pairedMetrics: censored first-30 round with failures at 31
final attack dice, spirits, and barrier
analysis.tests: Paired whole-game bootstrap with Bonferroni 98.333% simultaneous intervals for each family of three terminal-treatment claims. Exact two-sided McNemar p-values and Holm adjustment are reported as sensitivity analyses but are not an additional gate.

developmentGates.trainingParity: V31 anchor-only must be no worse than V30 initial by 1.0 percentage point, with paired 95% lower bound at least -3.0 points. Only this comparison can invalidate the causal interpretation of the compute-matched update.
developmentGates.v23Context: Report V30 initial and V31 anchor-only versus V23, but do not use those baseline gaps to invalidate the terminal-credit-versus-anchor causal test. V23 uses its native obs-v1 path while all V31 arms share the v2 binary path; therefore the clean causal comparison is treatment versus anchor-only.
developmentGates.terminalCreditCausal: Against V31 anchor-only on the same games, a treatment must gain at least +2.0 percentage points and have its Bonferroni-simultaneous 98.333% paired lower bound above zero across the three treatment claims.
developmentGates.strength: The same treatment must gain at least +3.0 percentage points versus V23 and have its Bonferroni-simultaneous 98.333% paired lower bound above zero across the three treatment-versus-V23 claims.
developmentGates.lateGame: Mean final VP and post-round-15 VP/round must not regress versus both anchor-only and V23; censored first-30 round must not increase. Report rather than hide guardian-specific regressions.
developmentGates.integrity: All evaluated reports must have the exact expected seed set, catalog hash, decode contract, checkpoint hash, 4096 games, and zero stalls.
developmentGates.winner: Among treatments passing every gate, select highest true-win rate; ties within one paired win prefer lower censored first-30 round, then higher post-15 pace, then lower coefficient. Freeze the winner before opening hidden seeds.

hiddenConfirmation.openOnlyIf: Exactly one V31 development winner has been frozen without using hidden data.
hiddenConfirmation.pass: Winner must beat V23 and V31 anchor-only by at least +3.0 percentage points with paired 95% lower bounds above zero and exact McNemar p < 0.05, have zero stalls, and not regress mean final VP, post-15 pace, or censored first-30 round. Absolute win rate is reported but is not a gate because hidden-block difficulty is unknown. This confirms a useful initialization but does not satisfy the final 80% goal and cannot promote.
hiddenConfirmation.failure: Reject offline terminal-credit checkpoint and initialize the next on-policy stage from V30 instead. Never test a V31 runner-up on hidden seeds.

nextStage.ifPass: Use the hidden-confirmed V31 winner as the initialization for a separately reviewed on-policy solo PPO/search-target stage on fresh seed block 946000000.
nextStage.ifNoDevelopmentWinner: Reject this offline one-step estimator and move to separately reviewed on-policy solo PPO or search-improved targets from V30 on seed block 946000000; do not sweep coefficients or epochs on 944000000.
nextStage.ifHiddenFailure: Move to the same on-policy stage from V30 and preserve the hidden failure as final evidence against this offline treatment.

stopRules: Do not consume 944000000 until training artifacts are frozen.
Do not consume 945000000 unless a sole development winner is frozen.
Do not tune from either development or hidden evaluation results.
Do not evaluate an invalid arm or a hidden runner-up.
Do not claim strength from offline loss or fidelity metrics alone.
Do not promote any V31 checkpoint.
